zh.lifeSummary: Agnes Prist於1526年出生在英國賀特福爾黑費爾德。她於1547年1月29日與William Wode在英國賀特福爾黑費爾德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1620年之前。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Agnes Prist于1526年出生在英国賀特福爾黑費爾德。她于1547年1月29日与William Wode在英国賀特福爾黑費爾德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1620年之前。

nameMeanings.name: Prist
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Constant, Alain, Francoise, Jean Pierre.

Dutch: variant and possibly also an Americanized form of Proost, from Middle Dutch proo(f)st ‘provost’, an occupational name for the head of a religious chapter or educational establishment, or, since such officials were usually clergy and celibate, a nickname for a self-important person.
French: shortened form of   Provost   1 ‘provost’, a cognate of 4.

variants.name: Provost
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Pierre, Andre, Emile, Camille, Lucien, Normand, Alphonse, Cecile, Gilles, Jacques.
French: northern and western variant of Prévost (see Prevost ) and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this (compare 2 below).

English: occupational name from Middle English provost, provest, proust ‘provost, steward, representative’ (Old English prafost), also used for the head of a religious chapter or educational establishment, or, since such officials were usually clergy and celibate, a nickname for a self-important person.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Prist was born in 1526, in Therfield, Hertfordshire, England. She married William Wode on 29 January 1547, in Therfield, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. She died before 1620.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnes Prist est née en 1526, à Therfield, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé William Wode le 29 janvier 1547, à Therfield, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée avant 1620.

de.lifeSummary: Agnes Prist wurde 1526 in Therfield, Hertfordshire, England geboren. Sie hat am 29. Januar 1547 in Therfield, Hertfordshire, England William Wode geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb vor 1620.

it.lifeSummary: Agnes Prist è nata il nel 1526 nel Therfield, Hertfordshire, England. Lei sposato William Wode il 29 gennaio 1547, nel Therfield, Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì prima del 1620.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Pristは1526年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスエルフィールドで生まれた。1547年1月29日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスエルフィールドでWilliam Wodeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1620年以前に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Prist(은)는 1526년 영국 허트퍼드실르 서르필드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1547년 1월 29일 영국 허트퍼드실르 서르필드에서 William Wode(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1620년 이전 사망했다.
